{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 嶽比良神社
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}{{jalink|伊豆村|Q11380579|伊豆}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 国柱命
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}論社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   =

|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=嶽比良神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}
}}
'''嶽比良神社'''（たきひらじんじゃ、たけひらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}東方の嶽ノ平(たき[け]のひら)山の山上付近に鎮座している。

{{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の3番目の子「やす」こと夜須命（やすのみこと）を祭神とする{{jalink|式内社}}である'''夜須命神社'''（やすのみことじんじゃ）に比定されているが、他の論社として、坪田地区の{{jalink|二宮神社 (三宅村)|Q134930277|二宮神社}}に合祀された御嶽神社がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱命（くにはしらのみこと）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 嶽比良神社
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}{{jalink|伊豆村|Q11380579|伊豆}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 国柱命
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}論社
|創建   = 不詳

|神事   =

{{coord2|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}
}}
'''嶽比良神社'''（たきひらじんじゃ、たけひらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}東方の嶽ノ平(たき[け]のひら)山の山上付近に鎮座している。

{{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の3番目の子「やす」こと夜須命（やすのみこと）を祭神とする{{jalink|式内社}}である'''夜須命神社'''（やすのみことじんじゃ）に比定されているが、他の論社として、坪田地区の{{jalink|二宮神社 (三宅村)|Q134930277|二宮神社}}に合祀された御嶽神社がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱命（くにはしらのみこと）

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 嶽比良神社
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}{{jalink|伊豆村|Q11380579|伊豆}}

{{coord2|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}
}}
'''嶽比良神社'''（たきひらじんじゃ、たけひらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}東方の嶽ノ平(たき[け]のひら)山の山上付近に鎮座している。

{{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の3番目の子「やす」こと夜須命（やすのみこと）を祭神とする{{jalink|式内社}}である'''夜須命神社'''（やすのみことじんじゃ）に比定されているが、他の論社として、坪田地区の{{jalink|二宮神社 (三宅村)|Q134930277|二宮神社}}に合祀された御嶽神社がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱命（くにはしらのみこと）

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}

'''嶽比良神社'''（たきひらじんじゃ、たけひらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}東方の嶽ノ平(たき[け]のひら)山の山上付近に鎮座している。

{{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の3番目の子「やす」こと夜須命（やすのみこと）を祭神とする{{jalink|式内社}}である'''夜須命神社'''（やすのみことじんじゃ）に比定されているが、他の論社として、坪田地区の{{jalink|二宮神社 (三宅村)|Q134930277|二宮神社}}に合祀された御嶽神社がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱命（くにはしらのみこと）

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''嶽比良神社'''（たきひらじんじゃ、たけひらじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}東方の嶽ノ平(たき[け]のひら)山の山上付近に鎮座している。

{{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の3番目の子「やす」こと夜須命（やすのみこと）を祭神とする{{jalink|式内社}}である'''夜須命神社'''（やすのみことじんじゃ）に比定されているが、他の論社として、坪田地区の{{jalink|二宮神社 (三宅村)|Q134930277|二宮神社}}に合祀された御嶽神社がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱命（くにはしらのみこと）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = Takihira Shrine

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}
}}
'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{jalink|Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{jalink|Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

}}
'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{jalink|Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{jalink|Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{jalink|Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." '''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{jalink|Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{神社

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{jalink|Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{神社

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{神社
|名称 = Takihira Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} {{ill|lt=Izu|Q11380579|WD=Q11380579|lt=Izu}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = Takihira Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} {{ill|lt=Izu|Q11380579|WD=Q11380579|lt=Izu|1=Izu}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神 = Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{coord2|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}
}}
'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} {{ill|lt=Izu|Q11380579|WD=Q11380579|lt=Izu|1=Izu}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}
}}
'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Takihira Shrine'''|嶽比良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = Takihira Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} {{ill|lt=Izu|Q11380579|WD=Q11380579|lt=Izu|1=Izu}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E|display=inline,title}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|37.2|N|139|30|20.3|E}}}}
}}
'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashira no Mikoto

'''Takihira Shrine''' (takihira-jinja, takehira-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}<ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140118-01.html 嶽比良神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref>[https://info.gbiz.go.jp/hojin/ichiran?hojinBango=5010005009460 嶽比良神社] - 経済産業省</ref>.

The shrine is situated near the summit of Takenohira (also read Takinohira) Mountain, in the mountains of the Izu district in the northwestern part of Miyakejima, east of {{ill|lt=Omatsuri Shrine|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}.

It is identified as '''Yasunomikoto Shrine''' (yasunomikoto-jinja), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} dedicated to Yasu no Mikoto, the third child among the eight princes born to {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Oshaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}), who is known as "Yasu." However, another Ronsha candidate is the Ontake Shrine, which was merged into {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|Q134930277|WD=Q134930277|lt=Ninomiya Shrine|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Q134930277)}} in the Tsubota district<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 p.84] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.
